summary: No CLAUDE.md or AGENTS.md standards files exist in this repository. A recursive search of /Users/mae/Documents/humanise (excluding .git and node_modules) returned zero matches at root, in humanise/, dev/, .claude/, .github/, or any subdirectory. The only repo-level meta files are README.md, LICENCE, dev/TESTING.md, dev/ISSUES.md, and .claude/settings.json. None of these define authoring conventions for skills, references, naming, frontmatter, reference inclusion modes, cross-platform tool policies, or commit conventions that the diff could be audited against. Per reviewer policy, no findings are fabricated against unwritten standards.

residual_risks.description: Repository has no CLAUDE.md or AGENTS.md. Skill authoring conventions (frontmatter shape, reference inclusion mode, naming, cross-platform tool selection, agent reference syntax, protected-artifact policy) are not codified in-repo. Consequently this PR cannot be audited for standards compliance and future contributors have no written rules to follow. Recommend adding a top-level AGENTS.md or CLAUDE.md if you want this reviewer (or analogous lints) to enforce conventions on subsequent diffs.
residual_risks.evidence: find /Users/mae/Documents/humanise -type f \( -iname CLAUDE.md -o -iname AGENTS.md \) returned no results.

residual_risks.description: dev/TESTING.md and dev/ISSUES.md describe testing methodology, grader bug history, and Anthropic skill best-practices compliance notes, but they are not authored as enforceable project standards. The 'SKILL.md best practices compliance' section in ISSUES.md cites Anthropic's external best-practices doc rather than codifying repo-local rules, so it cannot serve as the cited rule for findings under this reviewer's evidence requirements.
